desc:
Rows upon rows of slow rolling hills, covered in a thick carpet of grass,
stretch ever outward in all directions, save north - where the running of water
over rocks can be faintly heard, out of the steep rocky mountains. A rich
woodland grove sprouts up to the west. The hills flatten out into plains as they
stretch south.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The golden hue of short-cropped grass, rolling over wave upon wave of hill after
hill, continues outward in all directions. A grove of trees stands to the
north-west, while rolling plains and foothills continue north and east, until
the Misty Mountains. The hills flatten out southwards, joining the grassland.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The trail moves alongside the tiny brook to the south, paralleling the line of
the towering Misties to the north. All around lies the giant expanse of plains,
broken only occasionally by small groves of trees and some solitary hills. The
path continues through the plains, the link between the Dunnish villages.
name: A Faint Trail

desc:
Passing from east to west across lightly wooded plains, this faint trail is
sometimes impossible to find due to lack of use. However, it appears to widen to
the west, showing signs of recent activity there at least. Grassy plains
dominate the landscape here, with a large copse of trees to the north forming
the exception.
name: A Faint Trail

desc:
Constant winds, drifting down from the Misty Mountains, blow across this trail
kicking up dust into the eyes of travellers. The trail bends here, heading north
and east. The confluence of two streams lies to the west, passing by a
Dunlending settlement on its north bank.
name: A Faint Trail

desc:
Gurgling as it runs by the village, the stream is joined by a small tributary
from under the bridge northward. The water of this stream runs slow and deep,
widening also. Aside from the sound of water lapping against stones, and the
wind drifting across the water, this place can be rather quiet. A trail lies to
the east, along the water's edge. South of here a large forest rises up,
stretching south for many miles.
name: Fork in the Stream

desc:
A small trail leading east from a Dunlending village bends southward, narrowing
here due to infrequent use. A gently flowing stream lies beside the trail, but
it bends to the north into a wooded grove of trees. Trees can be seen in all
directions, threatening to join the larger forest to the southwest and consume
these plains.
name: A Faint Trail beside a Narrowing Stream

desc:
Standing in the midst of a large copse of trees, little can be discerned about
the outlying areas. Large conifers rise up around you, dropping their cones onto
travellers as a gentle breeze blows through the forest. Animals of all shapes
and sizes can be seen darting back and forth among the trees.
name: Copse of Trees

desc:
The hills here are relatively small compared to the massive peaks of the Misty
Mountains in the northeast, but are high enough to provide a good view of the
land. A rich woodland grove sprouts up to the west, while rolling plains and
foothills continue east, until they reach the base of the mountains. The sound
of running water echoes to the north.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
A lush, green plain of short green grass grows here and stretches far north into
the endless plains. Far to the north and east the horizon is dominated
completely by the vastness of the Misty Mountains. The grass grows higher to the
west, while a well-worn trail lies south of here running east to west.
name: Boundless Meadow

desc:
A never-ending expanse of fertile green plains unfolds before you. This
grassland meadow stretches far to the west and north, where it appears to stop
at the base of the Misty Mountains. The plains continue to the west, while a
well-worn trail lies to the south, bending north.
name: Boundless Meadow

desc:
A grove of towering conifer trees yawns over you to the east, while a lush green
meadow lies to the west, this well travelled trail slicing down the middle. The
pathway runs north from here, where it bends west, and to the south where it
appears to intersect another path.
name: Dunland Trail

desc:
Large, looming conifer trees push close together, slowing progress in every
direction. A carpet of lush green vegetation clings to the earth. The trees
appear to thin out toward the west, while densely continuing east and north. A
low stone wall prevents progress any further south.
name: Woodland Grove

desc:
Standing amid a towering grove of pine trees, one might feel rather small in
comparison. Water, cascading through tree roots and rocks can be heard
throughout this grove, as the brook makes its way north. The trees appear to
thin to the east and south, while densely continuing west and north.
name: Woodland Grove beside a Brook

desc:
Two well-trodden trails meet here, one moving in an east to west fashion, the
other coming from the north and dead-ending to form a three-way intersection.
Southward, the edge of a large forest can be seen and before it a stream,
fragmenting into several tributaries as it winds its way west across the plains.
To the east, a small Dunlending village lies by the river.
name: Crossing in the Trail

desc:
A stream running near the forest edge continues its path west, flowing in from
the Misties to the east. South of the stream an enormous forest takes root and
continues south as far as the eye can see. The stream appears to fork to the
east. A settlement can be seen to the northeast, surrounded by a primitive wall
of wooden stakes.
name: Stream beside a Forest

desc:
Standing on the forest edge, large conifer trees loom over you, threatening to
consume travellers who head south into the heart of the woods. Pinecones lie
strewn about the ground near the edge of a stream, which runs just north of
here. A trail runs on the other side, west to east, and looks fairly well used.
Both east and west of this location, the forest border continues.
name: Stream beside a Forest

desc:
This well-trodden trail, tracing next to a stream running east and west, winds
its way alongside a great expanse of plains to the north and a large forest to
the south. To the east it appears another trail crosses this one. Beyond that
smoke rises from a village.
name: Dunland Trail beside a Stream

desc:
Signs of recent use are evident everywhere along this trail, as footprints have
packed down the fertile soil into a well-worn path. A gently flowing stream
follows alongside the trail. Far to the east, faint wisps of smoke can be seen
rising up into the sky. To the north, expansive plains open before you while a
large forest begins to take hold to the south.
name: Dunland Trail beside a Stream

desc:
The forest begins to thicken immediately to the south, making travel difficult.
On the other hand, to the north a well-worn trail moves east to west beside a
gently flowing stream. Otherwise, the forest dominates the scene, its huge
conifer trees intimidating the inexperienced traveller. The forest border
continues both to the east and the west.
name: Forest Border

desc:
Tall and mighty conifer trees surround you near the forest edge. Pinecones lie
scattered about as squirrels scurry along gathering food and storing it in tree
trunks. The forest immediately thickens southward, while the wooded border
continues east. To the north a well-worn path travels east and west beside a
stream.
name: Forest Border

desc:
Standing at the forest's edge, the inexperienced traveller might reconsider
entering such a densely populated gathering of pine trees for fear of becoming
lost. The forest immediately begins to thicken to the south, while the forest
border continues eastward. To the north a well-worn path travels east and west
beside a stream, which bends west before heading south again.
name: Forest Border

desc:
Curving gently from the north to the west, the current of this slow moving
forest stream begins to quicken as other rivulets and tributaries join the flow.
The stream gives way to fertile plains to the north, while an enormous forest
begins to take hold south of here, stretching as far as the eye can see.
name: Forest Stream

desc:
Following a quiet stream south and east, this trail is hardly discernible, even
while being travelled. The stream appears to gather speed as it widens, south of
here. An endless sea of lush green plains unfolds to the north and a gentle
breeze coming from the Misty Mountains far to the northeast creates waves across
the waist high grasses.
name: Worn Path beside a Stream

desc:
A strong wind blows down from the mountains most of the time, across waves of
waist high grass, rippling across the plains. A worn path lies to the south
beside a stream, making its way east. An endless sea of lush, green plains
stretch out in all other directions. Far off to the northeast, the Misty
Mountains provide a backdrop to the plains.
name: Boundless Plains

desc:
Waist-high grass covers the plains, a deep lush carpet for the land. A worn path
lies to the south beside a stream, making its way west. An endless expanse of
fertile, green plains stretch out in all directions as far as the eye can see.
Far off to the northeast, the Misty Mountains provide a backdrop to the
flatlands.
name: Boundless Plains

desc:
Moving east and west, a worn path, closely following a gently flowing stream,
advances across a great span of plains to the north and an immense forest to the
south. A steady wind sweeps the ground at all times, kicking up dust into the
traveller's eyes.
name: Worn Path beside a Stream

desc:
Passing from east to west along a stream, the worn path widens as it moves
eastward. Vast open plains stretch far to the north, right up to the foot of the
Misty Mountains. A huge forest begins to take root to the south of the trail,
the towering trees drawing nourishment from the stream.
name: Widening Path beside a Stream

desc:
An endless sea of green grass stretches as far as the eye can see. Waves of it,
lush and thick, ripple as the wind blows across these plains. The grass grows
slightly higher and wilder to the west, intermingling with the occasional bush
and thick shrub. A worn path lies to the south beside a stream, making its way
west to the sea.
name: Boundless Plains

desc:
Deeply embedded scars mar the walls and roof, caused by the clawing of picks
over time. Some cuts are as deep as a dwarf's arm from elbow to wrist. The
treacherous slope would be near suicide to ascend except for the most expert of
climbers, being an almost vertical natural fissure. East is a junction in the
mines, with four tunnels running from it.
name: At the Bottom of a Long Shaft

desc:
Cobwebs hang from the top of this intersection, covering all but the door to the
east. The tiny spiders who spun them are not readily apparent, probably hiding
in the many small cracks in the stone. The web fragments are so frail now they
almost disintegrate when touched. South the tunnel dips downwards towards a
dead-end, while west and north the mining tunnels continue.
name: Mine Junction

desc:
Much of the dirt floor has turned to mud here. The walls, while keeping drier
than the floor, also have a moist muddy quality. The wooden posts that hold this
earthen tunnel in place have been weakened by the water seeping through from the
ceiling above. It seems to be a constant drip, gathering into a muddy pool near
the southern wall.
name: Muddy Dead-End

desc:
Although the roof and walls of this tunnel are normal for a Dwarven mine, being
of unpolished stone and wooden props, the floor has been smoothed into complete
flatness. West an iron door leads into the main mines, while east the tunnel
turns into a small chamber.
name: Smooth Black Tunnel

desc:
Cut to a smooth chamber, this cavern originally belonged to the Dwarven miners
who worked the caves. Certainly whoever now occupies it is no Dwarf, judging by
the paintings hanging here. There is various furniture around the room, and many
old scrolls and books. A table on the eastern wall holds various flasks,
although what is inside is not clear.
name: Black Cavern

desc:
The smell of horse manure, mixed with a musty earthen smell, assails the senses.
Feet cannot help but track this mixture with them wherever they go. Bits of hay
and feed lie strewn about the ground. The only exit appears to be south, through
a wooden gate.
name: Corral

desc:
Mounted on the walls are the heads of game, the chieftain's kills from nearby
areas. Crude maps painted with dye made from local flowers are hung on the east
wall, and several lie strewn about on a dark wood table, the dye being so faded
they are unreadable. In the northern corner is a hearth made of stone, filled
with wooden faggots. The only apparent exit lies to the south through a carved
wooden door.
name: Chieftain's Lodge

desc:
Hollowed out behind the map lies this small musty room, perhaps used to store
the captain's trophies or treasures. The earthen floor is smooth and flat,
showing signs of little or no use. The only exit from this room appears to be
back west, through the map hung on the wall.
name: Secret Cache

desc:
This tent show signs of being well used. A tanned hide drapes over the entrance
flap to the north. The crude wooden furniture laying about the room is carved
and simple. The focus of the room is a stone kiln, along the south wall, in
which many pieces of pottery have been fired. It is aglow with light, casting
shadows around the room.
name: Under a Hide Roof

desc:
A ramshackle wooden construction bridges the stream, allowing access to the
village's eastern half. Simplicity of design makes this bridge of wood beams,
knitted together with rope, very sturdy and functional. The beams show the wear
of heavy hoof marks, worn in over many years. The craftsmanship of the bridge is
not the most sophisticated, but still it is a source of pride for the
inhabitants of the small settlement.
name: Wooden Bridge

desc:
An underground stream rolls through the plains nearby, and some of its waters
are collected into a large pool here. Surrounding the pool a cart-worn dirt road
leads east and west. An impressive lodge stands due north, while small tents lie
to the south.
name: Village Square

desc:
A set of sturdy wooded guard posts mark the entrance to this village. The hoof
prints of many horses over time have made uneven dents in the ground. Bushels of
hay stacked near the posts lie half-eaten. Several cow-hide tents lie to the
south, the homes of this hillman people. A pinned horse corral lies due north,
little used by a people unused to riding.
name: Village Entrance

desc:
A glittering stream runs gently westwards over a green field along the ridged
side of a forest. The ice cold water murmurs as it changes direction around
boulders and sunken logs sticking up above the surface of the water. The river
bank to the south is isolated on both sides, bordered on one side by this river,
the other by a large boulder.
name: Forest Stream

desc:
The forest kneels down to the stream here, creating a rocky outcrop just above
the water. The scraggled bushes form a thick wall just before the steep bank,
while east an enormous fallen boulder blocks the way. Strangely, no other
boulders are anywhere in the vicinity. Far to the east the Misty Mountains can
be seen rising like titans above the forest.
name: By a Forest Stream

desc:
The ground gently slopes downward torwards a glittering stream to the south
where ferns and osier grow luxuriant on the damp ground, attracting swarms of
insects. A fresh wind coming from the open fields to the north makes the old
willows sway to the beat of the wind.
name: Stream Banks

desc:
This is the tent of the village chief, a large dwelling with several small
curtained rooms. It is barely furnished, with thick piles of furs providing the
only seating. In the centre hang a few hunting trophies of the chief's younger
days - deer antlers, boar tusks and even the skull of an orc. To the west a
large tent flap leads out into the village.
name: A Large Tent

desc:
The village centre is nothing special, merely a rounded area marked out with
white stones around a central fire. To the east lies the largest tent in the
settlement, an impressive dwelling of much wood and cow hide. South a tiny pier
ventures out over the river, allowing the natives to fish and clean themselves.
name: Village Centre

desc:
You are behind the only gate, a flimsy construction of many wooden stakes roped
together, of a small Dunlending settlement. A few tents stand on either side of
the track, homes to these people. The track leads to the south where the centre
of the village lies. Beyond it a river can be seen, where a small pier stands
poking out through town walls.
name: Inside the Gate

desc:
The plains gently slope downwards to the south where the ground is covered with
billowing grass. Sharp rocks and large boulders lie scattered on the ground,
impeding any movement. To the west a steep valley breaks the line of the plains,
but it is impossible to get down there from here due to the steep valley walls.
name: Sloping Plains

desc:
A camp isolated by the thick forest lies here. Animal skins are stretched on
racks, and in the centre of the camp sits a small fire with a blackened pot
suspended above it. Around the fire some logs have been laid as a primitive
seat. Even though bits and pieces of equipment lie around you the site still
seems to be organised after a fashion. A rocky scree to the east prevents access
to the forest beyond, and in the south thick brambles prevent travel.
name: At a Small Camp

desc:
A large overhang of rock creates a secluded area in the cliff, providing the
needed shelter for the forest's more delicate blooms to grow, and they thrive in
this environment. The forest floor is awash with colour. Bees buzz from flower
to flower ensuring this little garden's survival. The other reason for its
thriving flora and fauna is the seclusion - enormous fallen tree trunks block
the route to the south.
name: Under an Overhang

desc:
A great wall of pines surround you to the north and east, their spindly needles
covering the ground like a carpet of mottled green and brown. The trees are
massive, at least one hundred feet high. If you could manage to climb one of
these it would give a view of the forest for miles around. The trees are thinner
in the south, eventually opening up into a small clearing.
name: By a Wall of Pines

desc:
A small clearing opens up in front of you. The clearing cannot be more than
twenty feet across at its widest point but the lush grass does offer a place to
rest your weary feet or maybe set up camp. Tall thick conifers line the eastern
and western border of the clearing and are too thick to penetrate. In the south
a thick mass of bushes and briars are interwoven in a way that none save the
small woodland creatures may pass.
name: In a Small Clearing

desc:
The forest is thinner here than in other places but these huge trees still
manage to keep the forest floor in some sort of twilight. Small creatures such
as rabbits and squirrels can be seen giving you a wary look before darting away
into the undergrowth. Shrubs and bushes that live on the forest floor scramble
for any light that the canopy above them misses, which is very little light
indeed. A sheer wall of briars in the east blocks your venturing further that
way, while to the south a high cliff stops you exploring into the mountains.
name: Inside the Forest

desc:
The mountain climbs high above the forest here, giving an unobstructed view for
leagues around. To the north the Misty Mountains dominate the horizon, to the
west they drop down to lush plains and forests. Eastwards the mountains steepen,
a mass of near-vertical rock, and in the south large forests and vast plains
open up before you.
name: Mountain Peak

desc:
Anyone on this ridge is constantly buffeted by the wind crossing over the lip of
the corrie. To the east the ground falls away sharply before rising again to
another peak miles off in the distance. The ridge continues on to the north,
culminating in a large outcrop of rock forming the highest peak for leagues
around. To the south and west the land descends to mountain pasture.
name: Exposed Corrie

desc:
The grass becomes patchy at this altitude, giving way to patches of shingle and
rock. The tallest form of plant life barely reaches a few centimetres in the
sparse air. A narrow ridge to the north leads up to one of the peaks, standing
ominously over the trees. To the west the plains descend down to the forest.
name: Climbing Plains

desc:
The grassy plains are high above the forest here. The air is quite thin, making
every action more of a toil than routine. The plains continue to climb in the
north, where a steep ridge begins some distance away. Small flowers that thrive
in the clean, fresh air are in abundance, their coloured blooms poking above the
light grass. The Misty Mountains in the east and north form a sawtooth across
the horizon, tall and impassable.
name: Raised Plains

desc:
These flat, high plains are somewhat grassier than those to the south and
several kinds of wildflowers flourish in the lush, fertile soil. The sound of
small animals running through the grass reaches your ears and you spy the
occasional bird from time to time, looking for grasshoppers hiding within the
vegetation. Fields begin to stretch off to the east and the outskirts of a
forest are directly to your west.
name: High Plains

desc:
These high plains are covered by broad-leaved plants, and quite a few delicate
flowers. Low bushes and trees are scattered across the fertile soil, providing
shelter from the winds that often sweep across. A small campfire has been laid
here, surrounded by log-seats. Not far to the east the Misty Mountains can be
seen, towering high above the otherwise flat lands.
name: High Plains

desc:
This is a small wooden building, seemingly inhabited by several people. There
are a few beds on the southern wall covered by brown blankets. A wooden table
stands by the eastern wall, just beneath a small window. Just inside the door
several tools and even a few a weapons lie on a rack mounted on the wall. There
is a small fire crackling in the stone chimney, with a few pots and cooking
tools beside it.
name: A Wooden House

desc:
A high palisade surrounds this small village, seemingly put here to defend the
area against wild animals and any other creatures that might try attack the
settlement. The village lies on the very slopes of the Misty Mountains, just
beneath the steep rock faces. Northeast of here a stone well marks the centre of
the village, while west a trail goes through the palisade.
name: Inside the Palisade

desc:
A few small animals are running freely, looking for food. A few houses lie
scattered within the wooden palisade that surrounds the village, built just
beneath the steep stone walls of the Misty Mountains. Just south of here a door
leads into one of them, a small thatched construction made of wooden logs. A
stone well can be seen to the north, at the very centre of the village.
name: In a Small Village

desc:
This is a small village lying just below the steep slopes of the Misty Mountains
to the south. A high palisade has been built around it to keep wild animals and
the mountain orcs out. Just north of here the northeast part of the village has
been fenced in for cattle, while west lie a few wooden homes.
name: In a Small Village

desc:
A small part of the village has been put aside for cattle. A low fence against
the palisade pens them in, while they are not out grazing on the pastures near
the village. The ground here is crushed and muddy, without much grass. One can
see where the villagers have been feeding the animals, as the southern part of
the pen has the look of much-trampled hay.
name: Cattle Pen

desc:
A well marks the centre of this small village. The few wooden houses around here
are built of wood, with stone base to make them more stable. They are encircled
by a tall wooden palisade in all directions but south, where the Misty Mountains
provide an impassable barrier. The northeast corner has been fenced in, to keep
cattle in one place. There are a few hens running around here, scrutinising the
ground for anything edible.
name: Centre of a Small Village

desc:
Here, inside a small Dunlending village, a few hens are slowly walking around
looking for food. There are quite a few wooden houses spread out inside the
palisade, small puffs of smoke coming from a few chimneys. The wall surrounding
the village is about three yards high and seems very sturdy. Just east of here
the centre of the village is marked by a stone well.
name: In a Small Village

desc:
Here, inside a simple wooden house, there isn't much to see. A few wooden pieces
of furniture are placed around the room to make it more comfortable and many
pelts and blankets are lain on the beds. A tiny wooden chimney has been built in
the northeast corner, a fire crackling in its hearth. Several pots and jars
stand on a shelf on the western wall, and a wooden door leads out to the south.
name: A Simple House

desc:
The tunnel is narrow here, with broad shelves cut into the rock on either side
of you. Upon closer inspection these are found to be graves. Many disjointed
skeletons lie on each shelf, filled from floor to ceiling. A faint but eerie
wind emanates from the east, down the deathly dark aisle. To the east lies a
smaller chamber, but it is too dark to see any more.
name: Shelved Tunnel

desc:
Shelves line this room from floor to ceiling in this seemingly long unused
mausoleum. A thick dust has settled on almost every surface, and even the motion
of your feet puffs up great clouds of it. In each shelf there lie the bones of a
man, some have decayed weapons lying beside them, but most just lie with their
arms folded across their chests.
name: Shelved Room

desc:
Inside this tomb, there are no burial shelves on the walls as elsewhere, only
paintings depicting the life and conquests of its owner. In the centre of the
room stands a large, stone coffin with a heavy stone lid. The shadows seem to
move in the gloom, shifting from wall to wall. This seems to be an important
room as colour has been incorporated into the wall paintings.
name: Private Tomb

desc:
Inside this tomb are no burial shelves, only paintings depicting the owner's
life and conquests. The exception is to the north, where the is a single empty
shelf. In the centre stands a large coffin with a heavy stone lid. The shadows
seem to move in the gloom, shifting from wall to wall. This seems to have been
an important room, as colour has been incorporated into the wall paintings.
name: Private Tomb

desc:
The room is a perfect rectangle cut out of the surrounding rock. Many strange
runes and glyphs cover the walls almost as though they convey some sort of
story. They are arranged in neat columns running from floor to ceiling covering
each of the four walls. On closer inspection of the glyphs the figure of a huge
lidless eye appears frequently. In the centre of the room is a huge sacrificial
altar, hewn from a strange, black stone - it seems to absorb all the available
light. In its very centre is a prominent small hole, with many small grooves
leading out from it.
name: Altar Room

desc:
You have entered a small chamber, covering the walls from floor to ceiling are
many more of the hieroglyphs that cover the rest of this temple. The only
difference with these is that the glyphs and symbols here seem to have a much
darker and grisly nature. Frequently there are symbols depicting a lidless eye
and wars with extreme bloodshed.
name: A Small Chamber

desc:
Low, thick grass covers the ground, with small trails made by horses, cows or
some other kind of large creature here and there. The land has obviously been
used for feeding cattle; however there is no village to be seen nearby. A
watering hole has been dug in the shadows of a clump of aspen trees, partly
hidden by low bushes, but easily found due to the number of tracks leading into
it. The Misty Mountains are looming east of here, their foothills climbing
swiftly into vast peaks.
name: Green Fields

desc:
Here, just southwest of the Misty Mountains, the grass is verdantly green and
the low vegetation flourishing. There seems to be an abundance of small game
living among the low shrubs and wind-whipped trees. Several tracks can be seen
in the dark soil and a number of small farms can be seen just south of here.
name: Lower Slopes of the Misty Mountains

desc:
The wild vegetation flourishes to a degree here, but seldom grows higher than a
few feet above ground due to the biting winds. The white peaks of the Misties
curve around north and east of here, like blinking bejewelled crystals just out
of reach. South of here the contours of a few farmhouses can be seen on a small
knoll.
name: Lower Slopes of the Misty Mountains

desc:
Rising high above the flat prairie of Dunland, the Misty Mountains are an
impenetrable obstacle for any normal creatures to cross. Their white tops
glimmer in the clouds far away, and an occasional bird can barely be discerned
as a spot circling them. Here, on the lower slopes of the Misties, the low
vegetation is flourishing. It is possible to go south or west from here.
name: Lower Slopes of the Misty Mountains

desc:
You are inside a primitive farmhouse made of logs. A number of wooden benches
have been put along the eastern wall, partly covered by colourfully-patterned
blankets. There are windows in every wall, and a wooden door in the southern
side leading outside. The floor is made of oak planks, which crack quietly as
you move around. There are many jars and pots around the small fireplace in the
northwest corner of the building, containing food and plants.
name: Inside a Farm House

desc:
These green and fertile plains are covered by a broad-leaved grass, with many
small trails going in all directions. By the crushed swathes of grass the land
is probably being used for feeding cattle. The slow murmur of a stream working
its way downstream can be heard from the south, and some buildings can be seen
in the east. The small hills seem to grow west of here, making the land rise a
bit.
name: Green Fields

desc:
Here, in the middle of the Dunland plains, a few small farmsteads have been
built. Surrounding them are wind-beaten oak trees, and just south a small stream
running from east to west. Beyond the stream a high palisade can just be
discerned, with a few houses and farms visible within. The ground here has been
laid bare in several places, perhaps caused by the cattle on their way to the
water. A small knoll rises east of here, and the shapes of a few buildings can
be seen on it.
name: A Few Farmhouses on the Grassy Plains

desc:
Many large tents have been raised on this grassy knoll, amidst the ruins of a
farmhouse. In the centre of the tents there is a large fireplace, probably the
meeting spot for these farmers. A small herd of cattle is fenced inside on the
slopes of the hill, and many farming tools lie scattered all around. It is
possible to see for miles from this place, all around the giant swept arc of the
Misties that encloses these plains.
name: A Hill with Tents and Ruins

desc:
Several deep tracks of animals can be seen in the soft soil here, probably made
by cattle from the farms on a small hill to the west. This seems to be part of a
large valley cutting east into the Misty Mountains, while west of here the
vastness of Enedwaith stretches out seemingly forever. Just visible beneath a
layer of overhanging vegetation is the small line of a stream, coming from the
east and turning south.
name: Green Fields

desc:
A small stream gurgles by here, its waters nourishing the bushes and trees that
spring up on both banks. The Misty Mountains rise high in all directions but
west, where the plains of Dunland run to the horizon. Here and there lie large
stone blocks, littering the ground as if some giant had cast them down from the
mountains. There are many signs of this area being used for cattle, with trails
of crushed grass crossing the grassy knolls.
name: Lower Slopes of the Misty Mountains

desc:
Here, at the base of the Misty Mountains, the soil is sandy and the vegetation
sparse. Some low trees and bushes are scattered around in patches, growing a bit
more dense around the stone blocks that spot the area. North the Misties tower
high, while south lies a small hill covered by ruins. The only real spark of
life here comes from a cold line of water, running down a creek in the cliff
wall some distance to the east, and continuing through the plains.
name: Fields beneath the Misty Mountains

desc:
The craggy peaks of the mountains begin north and east of here, turning from
faint foothills into an utterly unclimbable cliff in the space of a hundred
yards. From the rocks to the east comes tumbling down a small line of water,
sparkling against the dark mountain rock. On hitting the plains it pools for a
moment, then swiftly disappears into the undergrowth.
name: Before a Vast Cliff

desc:
These plains lie slightly higher than the surroundings, making it possible to
get a decent view of the nearby landscape. A small stream meanders through the
landscape a mile south of here, surrounded by a corridor of bushes and fairly
tall trees. Further south the Misty Mountains rise into the sky, impassable even
for the most daring traveller. The ground slopes mildly downwards to the east
and south, providing an easy descent.
name: High Plains

desc:
The high, flat plains spread out before you and continue on to the north,
fertile and teeming with small wildlife. A mixture of short grass and tall
grains grows in abundance here, providing the perfect hiding place for rabbits
and small birds. The plains themselves are very level and higher than the
surrounding fields and forest, allowing a clear view for several miles in all
directions.
name: High Plains

desc:
Still following side by side with the stream to the north, the path continues on
west-east. Flat and well beaten down in this area, one finds travel on the path
here very easy and comfortable. A large expanse of plains begins to open up
further to the east and a flat track is clearly visible to the south.
name: Footpath by a Stream

desc:
Continuing on in a east-west fashion, the stream flows between a footpath that
turns to the south and an expanse of high plains that rise to the north, They
seem to be quite level and could probably offer a good view of the surrounding
land.
name: Stream at a Turn of the Footpath

desc:
Almost hidden among the tall plains grasses to the east, this tiny trail becomes
an real track here to continue on south. A settlement is barely visible in that
direction and this flat track appears to lead straight to it. An area of grassy
flatland opens up to the west and another larger footpath can be seen when you
look north.
name: Flat Track

desc:
A track leading from the settlement to the south and heading north crosses the
path that heads west through the plains and hills in that direction. Judging by
the marks alongside the road, this intersection is in constant use. The
settlement that is visible to the south seems to be quite large and
well-established for such a remote area and one gets the idea its inhabitants
must be quite tough to survive so far out from civilization.
name: A Meeting of Trails

desc:
The thin trail meanders through the grassy hills, surrounded by low trees and
green-grey bushes. The trail itself is so worn down by frequent cattle movement
that it lies half a foot below the soft surrounding soil. Many stones have been
laid bare in the light brown soil, making it somewhat troublesome to travel on
the route. South of here the Misty Mountains create an impassable barrier, even
for the most daring climber.
name: Thin Trail through the Plains

desc:
A well-travelled trail leads through the flat plains, a couple of feet wide,
going in a straight west-east direction. Another path, small and thin, branches
off to the south, where the plains rise up and become hillier. The grass which
surrounds it is lush and broad-leaved, with a few flowers giving some colour. A
quiet trickling sound can be heard to the north, a small stream flowing through
the grass. South-east are the Misty Mountains, the greatest mountain range in
all of Middle-earth.
name: Footpath through the Plains

desc:
The trail goes in an east-west direction. It is a few feet wide here, and seems
to become even larger to the east. The surroundings are quite flat, with a few
hills scattered around you. Here and there traces of farming can be seen - near
the trail the grass has been gnawed on by creatures and an occasional pile of
dung can be seen. The murmuring sounds of a small stream can be heard north of
here, bubbling and chortling over a stony bed.
name: A Wide Footpath through the Plains

desc:
A small trail comes through the palisade west of here and runs due east. It is a
couple of feet wide, and frequent travel has bared the earth of its green grass
covering. Above the palisade west of here a few chimneys can be seen, smoke
coming out of some of them. To the south the vast Misty Mountains hinder anyone
from travelling in that direction, while to the north the silent murmur of water
can be heard.
name: Small Trail through Green Fields

desc:
This little line of a stream is running from east to west, surrounded by bushes
and trees. Birds can be heard all around, but are very hard to actually see in
the abundant undergrowth. Many tracks of crushed vegetation can be seen leading
down to the water, the trails of large animals coming to drink at the water. A
few farm houses have been built on a knoll just north of here, their contours
visible against the sky.
name: Small Trickling Stream

desc:
A small trail that comes from the east turns quickly south here, following the
mountain base. The grass is thick and green, and there seem to be many animals
living in the small bushes. Just east of here it is a small stream falls from
the cliffs and flows through here, joining an overgrown brook from the north.
East lie some long abandoned ruins, simple farm buildings not used in many a
year.
name: Trail beside a Small Stream

desc:
A small stream appears out of the cliffs to the south a few yards up, and falls
to earth, before pooling and slowly flowing west to join with another stream.
Alongside the water are the last remnants of some buildings. There must have
been four houses here, clustered together in the shelter of the Misty Mountains.
To the east a small hill can be seen before the base of the main range of
mountains, a giant and never-ending series of white peaks.
name: Ruins near the Emergence of an Underground Stream

desc:
Ruins cover this low hill lies just beneath the main Misty Mountains. There
seems to have been a large number of small farms here at some point in the past,
before they were consumed by fire. There also seem to have been some kind of
stone palisade around the farmhouses, now only a crumbled yard or two high. The
remains of a recently used campfire are near the eastern wall.
name: A Hill with Some Ruins

desc:
Other than a pronounced slope from north to south, this track is quite wide and
comfortable to travel upon, leading straight into the settlement that lies a
little ways to the south. Running straight north-south, the track meets another
trail directly to the south and runs past the soft, grassy hills that rise and
fall gently to the west.
name: Sloping Track

desc:
The brightly white peaks of the Misty Mountains tower south and east. The
vegetation here is sparse, but near the base of the mountains low bushes and
trees take advantage of the shelter. An old campfire lies in a circle of small
stones, laid in a tight circle to prevent it from spreading. It is evident that
it is long since it was used last, the ashes almost erased by wind and rain.
name: Fields below the Misty Mountains

desc:
A small trail runs through the fields, along the base of the Misty Mountains,
turning west and north here to avoid the peaks. Even the best climber would be
defeated by those sheer cliffs which rise up several hundred feet. There are
many low bushes and small trees scattered in this area, providing shelter from
wind and snow. A slight murmuring sound can be heard north of here.
name: Small Trail through Green Fields

desc:
The fields here are partly burnt and the soil is bared in several places,
showing the light, sandy soil that covers the lower parts of the Misty
Mountains. The mountains tower up quickly in all directions but west, where a
small hill is raised like an outpost of the mountains. South many bushes grow on
the steep slope, crawling up the mountainsides and making passage difficult.
They seem oddly placed, almost as if someone has grown them to block the route.
name: Fields on the Lower Misty Mountains

desc:
A small stream rolls through the grassy plains here. A few large stones and
boulders have been bared by the force of nature and lie in its centre, the
waters cutting around them. Just south of here a palisade has been raised, many
feet high and with a few roofs visible behind it. The palisade lies before one
side of a spur of the Misty Mountains, casting a powerful shadow over the
settlement. North of here a few small farms can be seen, seemingly abandoned but
for a few animals standing amidst them.
name: Small Trickling Stream

desc:
Surrounded by flat land this trickling stream breaks the monotony of sparse
vegetation and scattered hills. It is bordered by flourishing plant life and a
few tall trees. The sandy soil is slowly being flushed westwards as the stream
gnaws on the land, meandering its way downstream. It is by no means hard to
cross the stream, since it is well under ten feet wide. South of here a giant
spur thrusts out from the Misty Mountains, towering over Dunland.
name: Trickling Stream in the Plains

desc:
A small stream is rushing downstream through the plains here. It is not even a
few feet deep yet, with the riverbed of smooth pebbles still visible. The water
itself is not hard to traverse, but will probably be less forgiving a few miles
west of here, as it gathers tributaries.
name: Trickling Stream in the Plains

desc:
You are on the slopes of a steep range of hills, a spur thrust out from the main
Misty Mountains to the east. Just north of here is the end of that spur, a rocky
outcrop like a pointing finger. Some way south, where the land drops to the
plains, is a large forest, and running from its western borders is a river.
name: Southern Slopes of the Misty Mountains

desc:
To the north a steep peak rises sharply above you, towering high into the sky
and dominating the landscape. A few small, sturdy bushes have managed to take
root in the rocky and barren soil, but otherwise only a low covering of grass
clings to the harsh surface. The rocky foothills of the mountains recede out to
the south and west, while the area becomes more hilly to the east as the ground
climbs slowly.
name: Below a Towering Cliff

desc:
The steep granite heights of the Misty Mountains are gradually transforming into
undulating foothills here, covered with rocks and boulders. Nevertheless it is
still a tiring route to travel, with long falls and sudden crevices at every
turn. The ground becomes flatter as the the slopes recede west and south of
here, reaching out into the plains of Dunland.
name: Foothills of the Misty Mountains

desc:
The steep mountain side is extremely hard to scale. A white peak looms above you
to the east while a deep valley, dotted with large rocks, can be seen far
beneath you to the west. A small hole, leading down into the darkness, can be
seen.
name: In the Mountain Heights

desc:
The tiny path twists through the bare landscape. These are the mighty Misty
Mountains, an almost dead place where nothing much lives. It seems very hard to
survive here, as the vegetation is sparse and there is no obvious evidence of
animals. Several peaks, their white tops glistening, rise high above this spot.
West of here the path winds between more peaks, slowly descending.
name: Path in the Misty Mountains

desc:
A rocky defile between two cliffs opens up here, one of many such small valleys
in the Misty Mountains. It is barren, cold enough to deprive it of vegetation
but not high enough to be snowed over most of the year. Through it runs a small
path, obviously barely used, which leads north out of the defile. It ends at a
metal gate, crusted with flaking orange rust, in the mountainside. There does
not appear to be any other route out of here, the exposed cliffs being far too
steep to climb.
name: In the Misty Mountains

desc:
The walls of this natural cave have been carelessly hewn, giving the impression
of a poorly skilled mason. The grey tiles covering the floor are decorated with
barely noticeable patterns, their crudity not diminished through the layers of
muck that cover them. There is a strong oaken table and some stools in one
corner of the cavern, where the guards gamble and arm-wrestle. A set of stairs
climbs up in the eastern wall, while west is a strong metal gate. Prominent on
the eastern wall is a drawing in gaudy colours: a large spiked club, dripping
with blood.
name: Neatly Carved Cave

desc:
The room is filled with the scent of leather, rising from the many battle-worn
pieces of armour and clothing that litter the ground. Weapons stand in racks and
shields hang on the wall, where they can be swiftly grabbed and taken into use
against any enemy. There is a narrow slit in the western wall, from where a path
outside can be seen.
name: A Guardroom

desc:
The only pieces of furniture in this room are shaky tables and creaking, rotten
wooden beds, covered with dark-brown stains and fresh blood. As the numerous
raiding parties leaving these caves do not always return in best health, the
severely wounded receive basic medical treatment here, although to many it
proves to be as deadly as any final blow in the battle.
name: A Crude Infirmary

desc:
Roaring flames occasionally burst out of the huge fireplace and lick the ceiling
of this ominous cavern. The heat is searing, hitting anyone who enters the room
like a physical blow. The reek of burning flesh hangs heavily in the air,
combining with the heat into a vicious stink. Several corpses lie piled
carelessly on a table, their bodies shattered in battle, waiting for their turn
to be consumed in fire.
name: The Crematory

desc:
Passages from the east and south meet here, forming a spacious cavern. As the
walls are not decorated nor finished, it is possible to make out the different
layers of rock that make up these mountains. It would be hard to spot the door
that stands at the far western side of the cavern, if not for the crude orkish
graffiti that surrounds and emphasises it.
name: Branching Tunnels

desc:
This corridor seems to have been carved by some underground torrent, now long
dried up or directed elsewhere, and later retouched by tools of workmen. The
passage continues to twist onwards west, and opens into a small cavern to the
south. The floor is covered with dirt and small rocks, making walking at any
speed treacherous.
name: Turn in the Corridor

desc:
This small cavern continues west, and narrows northwards to a tunnel, showing
signs of the long-calmed fury of an underground stream. Water drips from the
sharp stalagmites, forming clear pools on the floor. The sound of every drip is
amplified and echoed by the smooth walls, a soft cacophony of noise almost like
that of many people whispering.
name: At the Stalagmites

desc:
What first catches the eye in this tunnel is the grey tiling that paves the
floor. Although less than expertly laid, it is still a noticeable difference on
the rough and unpolished passageways and tunnels through the rest of this cave
system. Some scalps and other mutilated body parts hang on the wall at regular
intervals, becoming more skeletal to the east. The tunnel continues south also,
where the tiling ends, and opens into an intersection.
name: The Grey-Tiled Tunnel

desc:
The neatly paved passage continues west and north, where it becomes an junction
with a passage going west. In addition to the ominous trophies hanging on the
wall, several poles have been erected here, where victims of some arcane magic
hang - no normal weapon would rip its target apart with such ferocious strength
and explosive manner. Although sturdily built, the tunnel's ceiling has still
needed to be later supported with wooden beams. It appears that this part of the
passage is prone to forces that might tear it apart.
name: The Grey-Tiled Tunnel

desc:
This intersection has been decorated with a great amount of morbid care. There
is an eye-catchingly intricate carving on the eastern wall, and a pyramid of
skulls in the centre of the junction. The passage leading west is covered with a
narrow carpet woven of nothing else but pale and tender-looking skin. Various
ritualistic patterns have been drawn on the ceiling with soot and some other
dried dark matter, flaking away in most parts.
name: The Decorated Meeting of Tunnels

desc:
The tunnel makes a sharp turn and bends west and south. The grey tiles end
abruptly and a rocky surface extends from there on. Several cracks have formed
on the smooth walls, leaving the impression of forgotten care. A strong draft of
underground wind blows from the west, where the water flows towards.
name: A Ledge above an Underground River

desc:
The ledge ends here, the last fragments long since crumbled away. Below, the
fast-flowing water creates a strong draft of wind in the tunnel as it speeds on
westward. The only ways on from here are east along the ledge, or down into the
water and wherever it may carry you.
name: Above the Water

desc:
The smell of death hangs heavy in the air of this cavern. The chamber has many
different levels linked by rickety wooden staircases, and is rich in niches and
depressions. Torches high on the cavern walls cast an eerie flickering light,
placed deliberately to leave some areas in the shade. A dark pool lies steaming
and seething in the northern half of the cavern, the walls above it covered with
indecipherable symbols and drawings. Next to it is a black altar, cut from
marble-smooth black stone.
name: Hall of Rituals

desc:
The chamber is drowned in utter darkness - a darkness that seems somehow to be
more than a lack of light. The tiny fire in the middle of the room burns with a
bluish tinge, but casts no light. The symmetrical domed chamber above looks as
if it were burnt into solid rock - the surface is pitch black and completely
smooth. There are absolutely no furniture or decorations, only a circle of
leaves and fragile twigs around the fire. This is where the tribe's shaman
spends most of his time, drawing strength from some malevolent source.
name: The Chamber of Mordrath

desc:
A swift underground stream comes swiftly through a crack east of here, forcing
itself down a narrow passage. The water is extremely cold, drawn as it is from
the high snows the mountains above. It flows westward from here, hurdling rocks
and squeezing around natural rock formations, before dropping off in a sudden
fall into a northbound current. Above you can climb out of the stream, and onto
the end of a ledge.
name: An Underground River

desc:
The walls of this tomb are covered in strange runes and paintings. These are
barely legible now after the legions of time that have passed, but some shapes
and images can still be made out. Terrifying images of death and destruction,
but amidst the chaos a small party of various people can be made out surrounded
by what looks like a bright light. There is a dark passage leading off to the
east and stone steps leading up to the ceiling.
name: In an Underground Tomb

desc:
Iron bars have been erected to isolate this part of the cavern. Chains and
shackles are riveted to the walls, some of them holding only bones. These are
the living areas of the slaves, both captured human prisoners and occasionally
orcs of other tribes. Their labour is used to carve new caverns, and to mine the
poor lodes of iron. Not a single piece of furniture eases their life here, where
they have to watch the rats feeding on the bodies of their dead fellows.
name: The Slave Pen

desc:
These small intersections are nearly the only signs by which to orientate
yourself in these confusingly similar grey tunnels. Smoke from fires has made
the ceiling black with soot, indicating that the passage has been in use for
many years. Corridors branch in all quarters except west.
name: Branching Tunnels

desc:
Scalps and other mutilated trophies of reckless intruders decorate the walls of
the intersection, which divides into a network of tunnels. The floor is covered
with neatly placed grey tiles, much too well-laid to have been built by orkish
hands. Time and much passage has worn them, but the original pattern is still
very evident.
name: Entrance to the Tunnels

desc:
Above a gigantic natural fissure extends, the sky barely visible a long way
above. Among all the fallen rock debris some paths north and west have been
cleared to ease movement, but elsewhere it is a slow clamber over countless
stones. The walls bear signs of merciless tools, parts of the rock having been
crudely hacked away without much skill.
name: The Slanting Cavern

desc:
Here, far above the plains west of you, a chilly wind blows through the thin
air. It is extremely hard to travel here and the rocks and boulders strewn on
the steep mountain side forces you to move slowly. The mountain peaks still loom
far above you east of here.
name: High in the Mountains

desc:
This tiny path is twisting through the high slopes of the Misty Mountains,
across ridges and through valleys. It is surrounded by low birches and bushes,
moss growing only a couple of yards up their stems. Rocks and large boulders are
strewn everywhere, sometimes causing the path itself to change direction.
name: Path on the Slope of the Misty Mountains

desc:
Once this earthen tunnel was mined, judging by the rotten wooden supports that
prop up its roof. Now it has flooded completely, a dense mixture of mud and
water which makes it hard to see any distance. Through the hazy water you can
just see a hole in the roof, and the main tunnel leading upwards to the north.
name: A Flooded Mining Tunnel

desc:
Scattered cloth and hides lay across a bed here, left unattended by a busy
seamstress. A stone hearth, aglow with flames, stands against the south wall,
casting shadows around the room. Crude wooden pots and pans hang on a mantle
over the hearth. The only exit appears to be a dried animal skin draped over the
entrance.
name: Dunlending Cottage

desc:
The Dwarven miners who carved these tunnels would despair at the state of
disrepair which they have fallen into. A heavy coating of dust lies over the
carefully piled stones they long ago removed from this corridor's walls. The
walls, however, show signs of recent attention. Deep pick marks have been gouged
into the rock, exposing ore and some other minerals.
name: Deep Corridor within the Mine

desc:
Clumps of recently moved rock and dirt are carelessly strewn about the floor of
this passage, in sharp contrast to some neater piles that seem to have laid
untouched for years. Deep chops made by a sharp pick are visible on both sides
of this tunnel. Clearly these random marks were not made by the mine's diligent
dwarven creators.
name: Deep Corridor within the Mine

desc:
This is a long natural fissure in the rock, near sheer and almost impossible to
climb. The dwarven miners never used this route, finding it too dangerous and
narrow to even construct a stairway. Below you can see the base of the fissure,
and above a tunnel leads east from a ledge.
name: Half-way Up a Natural Fissure

desc:
Its walls carved smooth by many millennia of waters, this rocky tube is about a
man's height in width. Fed by the melted snow of the Misty Mountains far above,
it hurtles you onward. Whatever its destination, there is nothing you can do
against this current.
name: A Fast-Flowing Underground Stream
